title: approvals — background jobs
description: Seven jobs cover the async surface of the engine. Two sweepers (ExpireOverdueApprovalsJob, DispatchApprovalRemindersJob) run on Horizon cron cadences to enforce the timeout + reminder contract. Two dispatchers (NotifyApproversJob, NotifyRequesterOfDecisionJob) fan out notifications on lifecycle events. One purger + one reconciler + one report-generator round out the schedule.

jobs.purpose: Sweeper — every 5 minutes finds `approval_instances` past expires_at with status='pending' and transitions each per its template's on_timeout behaviour. This is the ONLY code path that transitions a pending instance to a terminal state without a human approve/reject/withdraw.
steps: SELECT approval_instances WHERE status='pending' AND expires_at < now() ORDER BY expires_at ASC LIMIT config('approvals.expiration.sweep_batch_size', 100)
For each row: BEGIN transaction
Load approval_templates.on_timeout (via approval_template_id FK; template_version resolves to the pinned version)
Case 'expire': set status='expired' + fire ApprovalExpired(final_status='expired')
Case 'approve': synthesise a system-decision on every pending requirement (approver_id = ApprovalSystemUser); set status='approved' + executed_at=null + fire ApprovalGranted

Case 'escalate': resolve template.escalation_selector → new approver ids; append to each pending requirement.resolved_approvers; set expires_at = now() + template.expires_after_hours / 2; fire ApprovalEscalated
COMMIT
Fire ApprovalExpired event with final_status = 'expired' | 'approved'
jobs.notes: Runs unconditionally under the platform-wide sweep — no per-tenant batching (would leave a small tenant's overdue instance stuck if the batch loop stopped early). Batch size configurable; a spike of overdue instances processes across multiple invocations until drained.

jobs.purpose: Sweeper — hourly finds `approval_requirements` in status='pending' where the elapsed time since instance.requested_at (OR the last reminder for this approver, whichever is later) crosses a threshold from template.reminder_config.intervals_hours AND we haven't hit max_reminders. Dispatches ApprovalReminderNotification + writes approval_reminders row (dedup via composite unique).
steps: SELECT approval_requirements WHERE status='pending' JOIN approval_instances WHERE status='pending' AND expires_at > now()
For each pending requirement:
Load approval_templates.reminder_config
For each user_id in resolved_approvers:
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM approval_reminders WHERE approval_requirement_id=? AND approver_id=?
IF count >= max_reminders: SKIP (throttle guard)
Compute next_threshold_hours = reminder_config.intervals_hours[count]
IF NOW() - MAX(instance.requested_at, last_reminder.sent_at) >= next_threshold_hours:
INSERT approval_reminders (approver_id, requirement_id, reminder_number=count+1)
Dispatch ApprovalReminderNotification (mail + in_app + push per approver's notification prefs)
Fire ApprovalReminderDispatched
jobs.notes: The composite-unique constraint on `approval_reminders(requirement_id, approver_id, reminder_number)` is the de-dup seatbelt — even if this job double-runs, INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING keeps the dispatch idempotent. `approvals.throttle_reminders` middleware provides the same guard for on-demand test dispatches.

jobs.purpose: Daily — hard-deletes `approval_instances` in terminal states past the 90-day retention hold (rejected + expired + cancelled) OR past the 7-year executed hold (SOC 2 evidence). Cascades approval_requirements + approval_decisions + approval_reminders via FK.
steps: SELECT approval_instances WHERE (status IN ('rejected','expired','cancelled') AND updated_at < now() - INTERVAL '90 days') OR (status='executed' AND executed_at < now() - INTERVAL '2555 days')
For each: hard-delete (cascade)
Fire nothing — audit records already exist
jobs.notes: Never touches approval_templates or approval_template_approvers or approvable_actions — those are governance rows retained indefinitely.

jobs.purpose: Synchronous fanout listener on ApprovalRequested. Loads the instance's requirements, unions resolved_approvers across pending requirements (only sort_order=min groups initially), dispatches one ApprovalRequestedNotification per approver. Idempotent — the notification's dedup key is `(instance_id, approver_id)`.
steps: Load approval_instance
Load pending approval_requirements (sort_order = minimum sort_order across the instance's requirements)
Union resolved_approvers[]
For each approver_id: Notification::send(User::find(approver_id), new ApprovalRequestedNotification($instance))

jobs.purpose: Fires on ApprovalGranted / ApprovalRejected / ApprovalExpired / ApprovalCancelled. Dispatches the appropriate notification to the original requester.
steps: Load approval_instance + requester User

Pick notification class by status: approved → ApprovalGrantedNotification; rejected → ApprovalRejectedNotification; expired → ApprovalExpiredNotification; cancelled → ApprovalCancelledNotification (rare — this fires when an ADMIN cancels a pending flow on behalf of the requester)
Notification::send

jobs.purpose: Nightly — verifies invariants that observers enforce at write-time (defensive drift detection). (1) Exactly one is_active=true per (tenant, application, action_key, name). (2) Every approval_instance.approval_template_id resolves to an existing row. (3) resolved_approvers[] contains user_ids that still exist + are not soft-deleted. (4) Delegation-touched instances re-evaluated → dispatches supplementary reminders if a delegate changed since instance-create.
steps: Check invariant (1) — SELECT ... GROUP BY (tenant_id, application_id, action_key, name) HAVING SUM(is_active::int) > 1 → email platform-ops
Check invariant (2) — LEFT JOIN approval_templates ON instance.approval_template_id=template.id WHERE template.id IS NULL → email platform-ops (this is a corrupted-state signal)
Check invariant (3) — resolved_approvers contains user_ids ← LATERAL join to users; missing users get logged as 'approver_orphaned'

Delegation drift — for every pending requirement, re-run ApproverSelectorEngine::resolve() with today's delegations → if resolved_approvers[] differs, log 'delegation_drift' + dispatch ApprovalReminderNotification to the new delegate

jobs.purpose: On-demand — generates a compliance snapshot (SOC 2 CC6.3 + ISO 27001 A.9.2 evidence) for a period + filter set. Streams result to a signed URL via S3-compatible storage; email link to the requester when complete.
steps: Aggregate metrics per the GenerateAuditReportInput filters: totals_by_status, totals_by_action_key, approval_rate, median/p95 time_to_decision_hours, expired_rate, security_violations_count
Serialise to json OR csv per input.format
Upload to filesystem 'reports' disk with 7-day signed URL
Notify requester with the download link
